CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Aug-19-2009 Off Calendar

Default Prove-Up Hearing As To Defendant Cma Spa, Only.
Off Calendar Jul-21-2009 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Dept. 218, Comm. William R. Gargano, Clerk: Kevin Dougherty, CSR: Lois Jones, #3689.

William L. Verick, Esq. and David H. Williams, Esq. appeared for plaintiff Mateel Environmental Justice Foundation.
Christopher M. Crow, Investigator for plaintiff, was placed under oath and examined.
No appearance by defendant CMA SpA, the only remaining defendant in this action.
Default was entered against defendant CMA SpA on June 19, 2009.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of Plaintiff Mateel Environmental Justice Foundation, and against the Defaulted Defendant CMA SpA as follows:

Damages/Principal - $40,000.00.
Attorney's Fees - $38,804.00, for a total judgment in the amount of $78,804.00.

Additionally, within 90 days of entry of judgment, defendant CMA, SpA shall contact any California consumer that purchased an espresso machine that utilized leaded brass plumbing any time beginning one year before the complaint was filed in this action. (See paragraph 2 of the Judgment).

Judgment was submitted to and signed by the Court.
